cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in STEREO A COR2, and S in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, difficult to resolve in early frames but becoming more defined as it travels into the field-of-view. This front may be related to a slow, faint dimming in SDO AIA 193 as early as 2026-05-28T01:57Z centered around S33E28 south of AR 14436 (S16E29). Though early, that source could also relate to later CME/streamer blowout 2026-05-28T09:23Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the E in STEREO A COR2, and extremely faint in LASCO C2 only resolvable in running difference imagery. The source may be faint dimming in SDO AIA 193 as early as 2026-05-28T05:04Z centered around S38E18 south of AR 14436 (S16E27).

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between good visibility in STEREO A COR2 and poor visibility in SOHO LASCO C2, but still able to track a related leading edge between both for several frames.

cmes.note: Faint event observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 only, likely due to the faint feature. No direct source for this event is observed, and is likely far-sided from STEREO A as well as Earth.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only plane-of-sky taken at W149, with the spacecraft currently at W59.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Source is likely a far-sided filament eruption observed on/near the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 starting around 2026-05-28T06:15Z, also observed on/near the SW limb in GOES SUVI 284/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible well to the E in STEREO A COR, and as a faint partial halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 comprised of multiple overlapping features, clearly a complex structure. This front may be related to a slow, faint dimming in SDO AIA 193 as early as 2026-05-28T01:57Z centered around S33E28 south of AR 14436 (S16E29), which is more directly attributed to earlier CME: 2026-05-28T03:38Z but this front at T9:23Z resembles more of streamer blowout and could simply be outflow following from that first signature. No distinct eruptive activity is seen relating to this start time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 with good confidence in fit and leading edge, and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 with less confidence due to the leading edge being difficult to follow and appearing much less wide than COR2. The COR2 perspective is used for the width.

cmes.note: Faint event observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 only, likely due to the faint feature. No direct source for this event is observed, and is likely far-sided from STEREO A as well as Earth.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only plane-of-sky taken at W149, with the spacecraft currently at W59.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-05-28T19:53Z, and this event is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is an eruption near Active Region 14455 (N18E63) starting around 2026-05-28T19:30Z which exhibits northern deflection as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/195/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME, however prolonged moving/opening field lines are visible beyond the W limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-05-28T21:00Z which may be more associated with the northern lobe of this feature.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on the southern lobe of this CME feature. It is possible this is a separate CME from the northern lobe, appearing with different speeds.
These parameters are based on the northern lobe of this CME feature.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning around 2026-05-29T17:03Z. Some of the early propagation of this CME is missed in running difference imagery due to poor image quality. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely a small eruption centered around S24W58 best seen in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-05-29T16:06Z as some ejecta with significant westward deflection. There is an additional feature in STEREO A COR2 around the same time, but this is most likely related to a far-sided, different source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on visible source location and running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-05-29T17:09Z. This CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2. The source is most likely some ejecta seen from beyond the SW limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 around 2026-05-29T16:45Z. There is a similar feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 (CME: 2026-05-29T17:03Z) which was determined to be from a front-sided, separate source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of available triangulation and beyond-limb source.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C3 beginning around 2026-05-29T18:00Z. It is also seen to the N in SOHO LASCO C3 and to the NNE in STEREO A COR2 but is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely a failed filament eruption centered around N65W00. The filament can be seen lifting off and mostly falling back beginning around 2026-05-29T16:00Z in SDO AIA 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-05-29T20:24Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 but is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely field line movement beyond the NE limb best seen in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-05-29T19:43Z. The CME mostly dissipates before reaching the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to beyond limb source and lack of available triangulation.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to a small eruption centered near S10E15 starting around 2026-05-30T00:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/193/304, STEREO A EUVI 304, and GOES SUVI 131/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption centered near N25E65 which begins to lift off around 2026-05-29T23:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is observed over the northeast limb around this time in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a northwest partial halo in STEREO A COR2 imagery, and a northeast halo in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Although no evidence of an eruption was visible on the Earth-facing disk, the Solar Orbiter STIX instrument, which was located at longitude -155 at the time of this event, observed the equivalent of a ~C9 flare peaking at 2026-05-30T08:01Z. Additionally, STEREO Ahead observed an increase in the 13-100 MeV protons starting around 2026-05-30T09:00Z, with a peak value of 0.02 pfu/MeV observed at 2026-05-30T10:15Z. It is possible the source is related to former Active Region 14436, located at N18E150 at the time of this event, based on prior activity from this region when it was on the Earth-facing disk. Additionally, the triangulated CME measurement places the CME in the vicinity of former Active Region 14436.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Shock measurement tracking the faint, outermost portion based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the S in SOHO LASCO C2, and more faintly in LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and not in STEREO A COR2 with a data gap starting at 2026-05-30T20:38Z. The source is likely a prominence over the SW limb observed first in STEREO A EUVI 304 as early as 2026-05-30T15:15Z, and in SDO AIA 171 as field line movement starting around 2026-05-30T17:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 plane-of-sky. CME half width is slightly less than 10 degrees.

cmes.note: This CME appears to have a continuous outflow front to the NE with a more defined front starting around 2026-05-31T10:00Z. The source of this outflow/CME appears to be a filament eruption with moving/opening field lines starting around 2026-05-31T05:00Z in SDO/AIA 171/304 to the NE with full liftoff closer to 2026-05-31T07:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on triangulation and best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. These parameters reflect the more defined front within the outflow to the NE seen closer to 2026-05-31T10:00Z

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery for a few frames. The possible source is seen as moving/opening field lines to the SE in SDO/AIA 171 starting around 2026-05-31T09:49Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 frames.

cmes.note: This CME visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source of this CME is a filament eruption best seen in STEREO A EUVI 304 to the SW with associated brightening and in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting around 2026-05-31T11:15Z. Additional moving/opening field lines and views of the filament are seen in SDO/AIA and GOES SUVI wavelengths beyond the SW limb starting around 11:46Z. Based on STEREO A EUV imagery, it is possible the source area of this filament eruption was near former AR 14443 (~S17W110 to W118) at the time of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on triangulation and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-05-31T16:53Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME appears much wider in STEREO A COR2 than it does in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen from along the SW limb (approx. S30W145) in STEREO A EUVI 304 with lift-off beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T15:00Z. Field line opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 284 and SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-31T17:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this event is a small eruption of filament ejecta seen originating from Active Region 14447 (S17W64) beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T16:00Z as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304. This CME was likely heavily deflected to the South.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-31T18:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely connected to faint post eruptive arcades seen located at approx. N15W110 in STEREO A EUVI 195 beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T18:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-31T20:12Z. This CME fades before exiting the field of view in C2, and is therefore not visible in other coronagraphs. The source of this event is a small eruption of filament material from Active Region 14447 (S17W65) beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T19:08Z as seen in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-31T21:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-05-31T19:38Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen centered at approx. S40E50 beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T19:22Z with lift off at approx. 2026-05-31T20:12Z as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304. This filament was likely deflected to the North by a large coronal hole to the South of this eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Large, bright, loop CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-31T23:36Z. This event is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and is visible as a partial halo to the northwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source of this event is wide opening field lines seen originating from beyond the NE limb beginning at approx. 2026-05-31T22:57Z in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated shock measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
PRELIMINARY bulk plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
PRELIMINARY shock plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided and may be related to an eruption visible over the southwest limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-05-31T22:40Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to an eruption from AR 4455 (centered near N25E35) as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and near the northeast limb in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery starting around 2026-06-01T03:00Z. An opening of field lines is seen over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193, GOES SUVI 171/195/284, and STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery. Additionally, dimming is seen northeast of the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 193 imagery and post eruptive arcades begin to form around 2026-06-01T05:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery only. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 149 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is possible this event is observed to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, but it is unclear as there is primarily outflow following the preceding CME: 2026-06-01T04:00Z seen to the northwest. The source is unknown but likely far-sided as there are no clear source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -31 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-01T10:08Z. The source is likely far-sided but may be related to minor, continuous eruptive activity near the southwest limb around this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the uncertain source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-01T19:00Z. This CME is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. This is the slower and wider of two overlapping CMEs. There are a series of coronal signatures likely associated with these two CMEs. This first is some ejecta seen over the W limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-06-01T17:57Z. There are also some post-eruptive arcades best seen in STEREO A EUVI starting around 2026-06-01T18:55Z to the W of a small, SW coronal hole. Another coronal signature is a field line movement seen over the SW in STEREO A EUVI 195, GOES SUVI 284, and SDO AIA 171/193 beginning around 2026-06-01T18:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement of BULK feature based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on visible source location in STEREO A EUVI.
Measurement of SHOCK feature based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the WSW in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-06-01T19:09Z. This CME is also seen to the WSW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This is the narrower and faster of two overlapping CMEs. There are a series of coronal signatures likely associated with these two CMEs. This first is some ejecta seen over the W limb in SDO AIA 304 beginning around 2026-06-01T17:57Z. There are also some post-eruptive arcades best seen in STEREO A EUVI starting around 2026-06-01T18:55Z to the W of a small, SW coronal hole. Another coronal signature is a field line movement seen over the SW in STEREO A EUVI 195, GOES SUVI 284, and SDO AIA 171/193 beginning around 2026-06-01T18:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on visible source location in STEREO A EUVI.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-01T23:00Z. It is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely a filament eruption best seen near/over the W limb in SDO AIA 304 and GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-06-01T21:54Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation. Additionally, the exact source location cannot be visually confirmed due to its proximity to the W limb.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T03:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is a C1.8 flare from Active Region 14455 (N15E09) seen peaking at 2026-06-02T02:50Z in SDO AIA 131. A small jet of filament ejecta is visible in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 in association with this flare, and is heavily deflected to the North, likely due to this region sitting just North of a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T07:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a C3.1 flare from Active Region 14455 (N15E01) which peaked at 2026-06-02T06:59Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. A small jet of filament ejecta is visible in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 in association with this flare, and is heavily deflected to the North, likely due to this region sitting just North of a coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Bright loop CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T12:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME has a faint, diffuse, preceding feature, but not a shock, seen ahead of the main bulk, likely due to the nature of the source eruption. The source of this event is a very gradual filament eruption seen centered at approx. S10E75 as seen in GOES SUVI, STEREO A EUVI and SDO AIA 304. This filament becomes unstable beginning at approx. 2026-06-02T10:30Z and gradually rises off the limb, but does not begin to disconnect from the disk until approx. 2026-06-02T12:00Z. This earlier rise likely accounts for the diffuse feature seen in coronagraphs before the main bulk of the filament ejecta. Field line opening, dimming and post eruptive arcades are also visible in association with this eruption in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Bright CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-02T17:00Z. The source of this event is a filament eruption seen along or just beyond the NE limb at approx. N18 as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-06-02T16:43Z. Wide opening field lines are also visible in association with this eruption in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement of the BULK feature based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on the visible source over the NE limb.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East in STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-06-02T17:23Z. The CME is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is an M3.3 class flare from Active Region 14455 (N13W05) which peaked at 2026-06-02T16:50Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Opening field lines and dimming are also observed in association with this flare in SDO AIA 171, 193 and 211 with the dimming and opening facing Southward, likely indicating some Southern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative measurement based on limited frames from STEREO A COR2 and visible on-disk source.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-06-02T18:09Z. This feature is only seen in STEREO A COR2 because it is mostly likely obscured by the occulting disk in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely some additional outflow following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z as there is no independent source signature apart from continuous field line fluctuation following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z. This CME fades before reached the end of the FOV of STEREO A COR2.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative measurement based on limited imagery from STEREO A COR2 and approximate source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-02T19:48Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There are two source candidates for this CME. The first and most likely is some faint field line movement from beyond the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-06-02T17:30Z following the filament eruption associated with CME: 2026-06-02T17:00Z. The second and less likely candidate is some additional minor ejecta from AR 14455 following CME: 2026-06-02T17:23Z, but measurement indicates that this second source candidate is not a good fit.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and uncertain source.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first visible to the NE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 staring around 2026-06-02T23:00Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. This front could be additional outflow following CME: 2026-06-02T17:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and lack of definitive source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-02T23:24Z. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of available triangulation and lack of source candidate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-06-03T01:53Z. It is also faintly seen as a halo to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 but is partially obscured by the pylon. It is also seen as a halo to the NW in GOES CCOR-1. This CME is associated with an M9.3 class flare from AR 14455 (N13W10) that peaked at 2026-06-03T01:36Z. The flare can best be seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit of the wider shock feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
PRELIMINARY measurement based on best fit of early frames among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption visible on/near the western limb in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a partial halo to the north/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an M7.7 flare from Active Region 14455 (N14W13) starting around 2026-06-03T07:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/194/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. A large ejecta is associated with this event which is deflected to the northwest. Additionally, an EUV wave is observed moving northwest of the eruption site, as seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of the messy, two-lobed bulk front of this CME, based on the fit with three coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1). There is clear northward deflection from the source location of the M-class flare (N14W13) but the associated erupting filament seems to originate a bit to the west from the flare location and is then seen deflecting northwardly, so the coronal signatures generally confirm this analysis. The half-width of the CME is based on later imagery in STEREO A COR2, as in coronagraphs there is/are later, wider, lobe(s) of this CME that seem to be integral part of it.
Approximate analysis of the fainter and wider partial halo front best seen in GOES CCOR-1 but also seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and not seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.

cmes.note: This CME is visible as a partial halo to the north/northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an X1.0 flare and subsequent eruption from Active Region 14455 (N14W16) starting around 2026-06-03T11:20Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. The associated ejecta displays a southeast deflection as seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery. A faint EUV wave is observed moving northwest of the eruption site, however, in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: A remeasurement of the two-lobed bulk front with more imagery.

Approximate analysis of the faint partial halo shock front of this CME based on the fit with GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph, in which this front is seen clearer. LASCO imagery is also used. The front is not seen/clear in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.
Very preliminary, approximate analysis of the two-lobed bulk of the CME with very early imagery based on the fit in coronagraphs.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-03T14:02Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely some very faint moving field lines from well beyond the SW limb best seen in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-06-03T13:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-03T20:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-03T19:38Z. The source of this event is faint field line movement/opening seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-06-3T19:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using longitude of W90 due to source near the limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-03T23:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-06-03T19:38Z. The source of this event is a small eruption seen beginning at approx. 2026-06-3T22:30Z from the vicinity of Active Region 14446 (S15W73) as seen in SDO AIA 171/193/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.
